If that’s the case, I’ll make the selector explicit in the helper layer rather than broadening the test surface.

I’ve confirmed the conformance suite itself is already matrixed. The remaining gap is helper ergonomics: the JSONL path is still “default by omission,” so I’m going to make the selector explicit in the helper constructor and then wire the suite through that path.

I’m making the JSONL helper explicit and threading the conformance suites through that helper, which keeps the behavior the same but removes the “default by omission” path the bead called out. After that I’ll run the package tests and the pre-commit gate.

The harness change is in place. I’m formatting the touched files, then I’ll run `go test ./internal/bead/...` and the repo pre-commit hook so I can verify the matrix and the working tree gate together.
The package test run is still in flight. I’m polling for the result before I touch anything else so I can fix any failure in the same pass.
